explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 39 from a base nutrition score of 48. Context dragged it down by 9 points.

topPros.title: highly palatable and appealing
topPros.explanation: A real-world reason people love it.

topPros.title: works as a strong flavour booster
topPros.explanation: Useful in meals even in smaller amounts.

topPros.title: has strong comfort-food appeal
topPros.explanation: Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

topCons.title: sodium is a major drawback
topCons.explanation: A central practical health issue for bacon.

topCons.title: processing is a major negative
topCons.explanation: A key downside now that more processed foods are in scope.

topCons.title: fat profile is rough for the category
topCons.explanation: Hard to justify against stronger meats.

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for natural flow and trim any robotic lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.

nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: fat is 42g. saturated fat is a real drawback here. for meats, fat quality is one of the main tie-breakers after protein.
scenes.subtitleText: fat is 42g. saturated fat is a real drawback here. for meats, fat quality is one of the main tie-breakers after protein.

scenes.narrationText: carbs is 1.4g. carbs do not matter much here. meats usually live or die on protein quality and what comes with the fat.
scenes.subtitleText: carbs is 1.4g. carbs do not matter much here. meats usually live or die on protein quality and what comes with the fat.

scenes.narrationText: protein is 37g. the protein quality is strong. for meats, strong protein is expected, so the rest decides how high it climbs.

scenes.narrationText: vitamin b12 at 20% DV, vitamin d at 2% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the food very far on its own.
